scope.target: Promote requirement and spec authoring from prompt-guide-only to formal skill support
scope.dependency_radius: Skill assets, wrapper skills, and guide/index updates
constraints: Reuse existing requirement and spec guides as the human-facing truth
Keep the skills focused on authoring, not architecture redesign or implementation
Avoid introducing a second conflicting schema
non_goals: No runtime wrapper metadata beyond current repo conventions

inputs.architecture_target: Create a full documentation-authoring skill chain from requirement through problem frame
review_findings: Requirement and spec authoring had guides but no formal top-level skills
The user wants those transitions to be toolable and reusable across projects
user_constraints: Add the skills now rather than leaving them as guide-only
Clarify how the problem-frame skill and sub-agent should be used

steps: Create requirement-author skill assets and wrappers
Create spec-author skill assets and wrappers
Update indexes and strategy docs to match the new formal skill status
validation: The new skills point to the existing guide truth
The new skills have clear boundaries and handoff rules
Repo indexes list the skills as active
deferred_items: Possible future sub-agent prompts for requirement and spec authoring
Forward-testing these skills on imported real project documents

results.summary: Added formal requirement-author and spec-author skills, aligned them with existing guides, and updated repo indexes and strategy docs.
tests_run: Structural verification by file presence and index alignment
